page.title: Speedboat: Best Boat in Marbella for Tow-sports and watersports day

data.meta_description: Why a speedboat is best for tow-sports and watersports in Marbella. 6 pax, 7m, departs Puerto Banús. Book your day of action now.
data.summary: Our 7m speedboat (6 pax) is the best choice in Marbella for tow-sports and watersports, combining agility, speed, and a shallow draft for close-to-shore action. Departing from Puerto Banús, it offers an adrenaline-packed day on the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: 7m speedboat, maximum 6 passengers, departs Puerto Banús
Ideal for waterskiing, wakeboarding, tubing, and kneeboarding
Shallow draft allows access to coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés
Licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA included

faq.q: What makes a speedboat the best for tow-sports in Marbella?
faq.a: A speedboat's lightweight hull and powerful engine deliver the quick acceleration and tight turning needed for waterskiing, wakeboarding, and tubing. Its shallow draft also lets you get closer to the shore for calmer water in sheltered coves like Cala del Faro.

faq.q: How many people can the speedboat carry?
faq.a: The speedboat carries a maximum of 6 passengers including the skipper. That leaves 5 spots for your group – ideal for a small party where everyone gets plenty of time on the water.

faq.q: Is watersports equipment included in the charter?
faq.a: No, tow-sports equipment is not included in the base charter. You can hire waterskis, wakeboards, tubes, and kneeboards on request. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange equipment for your day.

faq.q: Where does the speedboat depart from?
faq.a: All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. The marina is easy to reach by car or taxi, and there is paid parking nearby.

faq.q: Can we combine tow-sports with a relaxing cruise?
faq.a: Absolutely. The speedboat is nimble enough for action-packed tow-sports but also comfortable for a slow cruise along the coast to spots like Cabopino or Estepona. You decide the balance.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Tow-sports equipment is extra.

faq.q: How do I book the speedboat for a watersports day?
faq.a: Booking is simple: contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date and duration. We will confirm availability and send you a quote. Pricing is on request – no fixed hourly rates.

faq.q: What is the best time of year for tow-sports in Marbella?
faq.a: April to October offers the warmest sea temperatures (18–25 °C) and calmest conditions. Early morning or late afternoon often has the flattest water, ideal for beginners learning to ski or wakeboard.

data.body_html:
If you are looking for the speedboat best for tow-sports and watersports in Marbella, our 7-metre, 6-passenger speedboat is the clear choice. Departing from Puerto Banús, this agile boat combines a powerful engine with a shallow draft, letting you zip into sheltered coves and pull a skier or tuber with ease. Whether you want to learn to wakeboard or just blast around the coast, this is the boat for an action-packed day on the water.

Why a speedboat is the best choice for watersports

Not every charter boat is built for tow-sports. A speedboat's lightweight hull and responsive steering give you the quick acceleration and tight turning needed for waterskiing, wakeboarding, and tubing. Our speedboat is purpose-designed for this kind of fun – it gets on plane fast, holds a steady wake, and handles like a sports car. Compare that to a heavy flybridge or motor yacht, which can feel sluggish and create a wash that is less forgiving for beginners.

Specs and capacity

  • Length: 7 metres
  • Maximum passengers: 6 (including skipper)
  • Departure point: Puerto Banús, Marbella
  • Engine power: Sufficient for waterskiing and wakeboarding at planing speeds
  • Draft: Shallow – ideal for accessing coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés

With a maximum of 6 people on board, your group gets plenty of time on the water without feeling crowded. The skipper handles all navigation and safety, so you can focus on the fun.

Tow-sports equipment – what you need to know

Tow-sports equipment is not included in the base charter price, but we can arrange hire for waterskis, wakeboards, tubes, kneeboards, and life jackets. Just let us know what you need when you book via WhatsApp. Prices vary depending on the equipment and duration. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance to guarantee availability.

Best coves and spots for watersports near Marbella

The Costa del Sol coastline offers plenty of sheltered waters perfect for tow-sports. From Puerto Banús, your skipper can take you to:

  • Cala del Faro – a quiet cove with flat water, great for beginners
  • Cala Cortés – slightly larger, with good depth for wakeboarding
  • Cabopino – a sandy bay with a protected inner area
  • Estepona – calmer waters further west, often less busy

The speedboat's shallow draft means you can get closer to shore than larger yachts, giving you access to these sheltered spots even in light wind.

What's included in every charter

All our charters come with a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden extras – just turn up and enjoy. Tow-sports equipment is the only additional cost.

How to book the speedboat for a watersports day

Booking is straightforward. Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, number of guests, and any equipment requests. We will confirm availability and send you a quote. Pricing is on request – we do not publish fixed hourly rates for this boat. For a quick idea of our other boats' pricing, check our main fleet page.

Frequently asked questions

What makes a speedboat the best for tow-sports in Marbella?

A speedboat's lightweight hull and powerful engine deliver the quick acceleration and tight turning needed for waterskiing, wakeboarding, and tubing. Its shallow draft also lets you get closer to the shore for calmer water in sheltered coves like Cala del Faro.

How many people can the speedboat carry?

The speedboat carries a maximum of 6 passengers including the skipper. That leaves 5 spots for your group – ideal for a small party where everyone gets plenty of time on the water.

Is watersports equipment included in the charter?

No, tow-sports equipment is not included in the base charter. You can hire waterskis, wakeboards, tubes, and kneeboards on request. Contact us via WhatsApp to arrange equipment for your day.

Where does the speedboat depart from?

All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. The marina is easy to reach by car or taxi, and there is paid parking nearby.

Can we combine tow-sports with a relaxing cruise?

Absolutely. The speedboat is nimble enough for action-packed tow-sports but also comfortable for a slow cruise along the coast to spots like Cabopino or Estepona. You decide the balance.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Tow-sports equipment is extra.

How do I book the speedboat for a watersports day?

Booking is simple: contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date and duration. We will confirm availability and send you a quote. Pricing is on request – no fixed hourly rates.

What is the best time of year for tow-sports in Marbella?

April to October offers the warmest sea temperatures (18–25 °C) and calmest conditions. Early morning or late afternoon often has the flattest water, ideal for beginners learning to ski or wakeboard.

Ready to hit the water? Browse our full fleet in Marbella or message us on WhatsApp to book the speedboat for your next watersports adventure.
